questions.question: What does 'personal development' most accurately mean for a care worker?
options: Continuing to build your knowledge, skills and behaviours throughout your working life
Completing your induction and then stopping formal learning
Only learning new tasks when your manager insists on it
Gaining a pay rise after each year of service

questions.question: The Care Certificate can be fully completed through e-learning alone, without any workplace observation.
options: True
False

questions.question: How many standards make up the Care Certificate?
For the Care Certificate to be achieved, what must happen in addition to gaining the underpinning knowledge?
options: The worker must pass a written exam set by CQC
An employer must assess and sign off observed competence in practice
The worker must complete a Level 3 diploma first
A colleague must verbally confirm the knowledge was learned

questions.question: Select all that apply. Which of the following are true of the Care Certificate?
options: It is made up of 16 standards
It requires both knowledge and assessed competence
An employer signs off that competence has been demonstrated
It is awarded automatically on completing an online course

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a job description?
options: To list every colleague you must report to
To set out your role, duties and the responsibilities expected of you
To record your annual leave entitlement
To replace the need for supervision

questions.question: What are 'agreed ways of working'?
options: Informal habits picked up from experienced colleagues
Your employer's policies and procedures that set out how tasks should be carried out
Personal preferences about how you like to work
Rules that only apply to senior staff

questions.question: Complete: Reflective ___ means thinking about your work in order to learn from it and improve.
A simple reflective model can be summarised as which sequence?
options: Describe, evaluate, plan
Record, report, repeat
Observe, ignore, continue
Plan, forget, review

questions.question: In a describe-evaluate-plan reflective model, what happens at the 'evaluate' stage?
options: You write down exactly what happened without judgement
You decide what you will do differently next time
You consider what went well and what could have gone better
You ask your manager to complete an incident form

questions.question: Reflecting on feedback and on things that did not go well is a valuable part of improving your practice.
options: True
False

questions.question: How is it best to respond to constructive feedback about your work?
options: Treat it as a personal attack and defend yourself
Ignore it unless it comes from your manager
Consider it openly and use it to improve your practice
Only accept it if it is entirely positive

questions.question: Which set of words best describes what good supervision should be?
options: Occasional, informal and public
Regular, supportive and confidential
Rare, disciplinary and recorded publicly
Unplanned, critical and anonymous

questions.question: Select all that apply. Which of the following are features of effective supervision?
options: It takes place regularly
It offers support and a chance to reflect
Discussions are kept confidential
It is used mainly to catch staff making mistakes

questions.question: What is the best way to prepare for a supervision session so you get the most from it?
options: Turn up with nothing planned and wait to be told what to discuss
Think in advance about issues, achievements and questions you want to raise
Only attend if there is a problem to report
Ask a colleague to attend in your place

questions.question: Complete: An ___ is usually an annual review of your overall performance and future objectives.
How does an appraisal typically differ from routine supervision?
options: An appraisal happens weekly, supervision happens yearly
An appraisal reviews overall performance over a longer period and sets objectives
An appraisal is confidential but supervision is not
There is no difference between them

questions.question: In the SMART approach to setting objectives, what does the 'M' stand for?
options: Manageable
Measurable
Motivating
Mandatory

questions.question: Select all that apply. Which words are part of the SMART framework for objectives?
options: Specific
Achievable
Relevant
Random

questions.question: A well-written SMART objective should include which of the following?
options: A vague aim with no deadline
A clear, measurable goal with a time frame to achieve it
A list of everyone else's responsibilities
Only objectives set by your manager without your input

questions.question: Complete: A personal ___ plan records your learning goals and how you will achieve them.
What is the main purpose of a personal development plan (PDP)?
options: To record complaints about your workplace
To set out your learning and development goals and how to reach them
To replace your job description
To track only your sickness absence

questions.question: A personal development plan should be reviewed and updated over time rather than written once and forgotten.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which statement best reflects how people learn?
options: Everyone learns best in exactly the same way
People have different learning styles and preferences
Learning only happens on formal training courses
Only younger staff are able to learn new skills

questions.question: Select all that apply. Who or what can be a source of support for your development at work?
options: Experienced colleagues
A mentor
Your line manager
Funded learning opportunities

questions.question: What is the role of a mentor in your development?
options: To carry out your duties for you
To offer guidance, encouragement and share their experience
To formally discipline you when you make errors
To set your rate of pay

questions.question: What is a portfolio of evidence used for?
options: Storing your personal shopping receipts
Collecting proof of your learning, competence and achievements
Recording other staff members' mistakes
Replacing the need for supervision meetings

questions.question: When keeping a portfolio that includes examples from your work, what must you do to protect people you support?
options: Include full names and addresses so it is accurate
Remove or anonymise personal information to protect confidentiality
Share it publicly to show your progress
Store it on a personal phone with no security

questions.question: Personal development is a one-off event completed during induction and not something that continues throughout your career.
options: True
False

questions.question: Why is ongoing personal development important in adult social care?
options: It guarantees a promotion every year
It helps keep your knowledge and skills current so you provide safe, good-quality care
It removes the need to follow agreed ways of working
It is only important for managers, not care workers
